steps.title: Identify the key signal: ambivalence
steps.content: The claim describes a contradiction—the speaker both acknowledges merit and expresses displeasure about poetry. The strongest signal is a quotation capturing this simultaneous dual attitude, not just criticism or praise alone.

steps.title: Match the complete signal
steps.content: D opens with “perfect contempt for” poetry but then discovers “a place for the genuine,” with “however” and “after all” marking the shift from dismissal to recognition. A, B, and C each present only skepticism or criticism without demonstrating any offsetting appreciation. That matches choice D — D is correct.
